title: Where the Compass Points
synopsis: High in the emerald canopy of the rainforest, Lily discovers an ancient map that promises to lead her to a hidden waterfall. But when the map proves wrong at a crucial moment, she must use her own observations and curiosity to find her way through the mysterious treetop world.

pages.text: The wind whispers secrets through the emerald leaves. Lily sits on a branch as thick as her bed. She is hundreds of feet above the ground. Something crinkles beneath her foot. She looks down. A piece of old paper peeks from under the moss. She picks it up carefully. It is a map. The edges are brown and torn. Strange symbols mark the paths. A red X glows like a jewel. "Waterfall," she whispers to herself.

illustration.description: Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, and green eyes crouches on a massive moss-covered branch, carefully lifting an aged parchment map from beneath green moss. The rainforest canopy stretches in all directions, dappled sunlight filtering through layers of emerald leaves. Drawn in treasure map style with sepia tones, dotted trails visible on the map, and compass rose details.
illustration.alt: A girl discovering an old map hidden in the moss of a rainforest branch

pages.text: She traces the dotted line with her finger. It starts where she sits. It curves through the canopy. It ends at the red X. The map shows rope bridges. It shows hollow trees. It shows places she has never seen. Her heart beats faster. The leaves rustle like old paper. The canopy holds its breath. She folds the map. She tucks it in her pocket. Tomorrow she will follow it.

illustration.description: Close-up of Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, and green eyes studying the aged parchment map. Her finger traces a dotted trail marked with compass roses and adventure symbols. The map shows rope bridges and hollow trees in hand-drawn cartography style. Emerald canopy light filters through, casting jewel-toned shadows on the treasure map.
illustration.alt: A girl examining the details of an ancient map showing forest paths

pages.text: Morning mist clings to the leaves. Lily pulls out the map. The first symbol shows a twisted branch. She looks around. There it is. Gnarled like an old finger. She climbs toward it. The map shows a rope bridge next. She finds it swaying in the breeze. Her feet test each wooden plank. They hold. She crosses slowly. The canopy opens like a green cathedral.

illustration.description: Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, and green eyes carefully crosses a rope bridge suspended between massive trees. Morning mist swirls through the emerald canopy below and around her. The scene is rendered in treasure map style with sepia tones and adventure cartography elements, showing the vast cathedral-like space of the rainforest canopy.
illustration.alt: A girl crossing a rope bridge high in the misty rainforest canopy

pages.text: She checks the map again. A hollow tree should be here. She finds it. Dark and deep like a secret tunnel. She crawls through. Moss tickles her arms. She emerges on the other side. The map shows one more bridge. Then the waterfall. But when she looks ahead, there is no bridge. Only empty air. Only a gap too wide to cross. The map is wrong.

illustration.description: Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, and green eyes stands at the edge of a thick branch, looking across a wide gap in the canopy where no bridge exists. She holds the aged parchment map in one hand, comparing it to the reality before her. The illustration uses treasure map style with dotted trails and compass elements, showing the emerald canopy stretching endlessly below the gap.
illustration.alt: A girl realizing the map doesn't match what she sees in the forest

pages.text: Lily sits on the branch. She listens. The canopy speaks in whispers. Water trickles somewhere. Not ahead where the map points. To the left. She tilts her head like a bird. Yes. Water over stones. The sound is faint. But it is there. She looks at the map. She looks at the forest. The map shows one way. Her ears hear another.

illustration.description: Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, and green eyes sits quietly on a branch, her head tilted in concentration as she listens to forest sounds. The aged parchment map lies beside her. Emerald leaves frame the scene, with subtle visual cues suggesting sound waves. Treasure map style illustration with sepia tones and jewel-green accents in the foliage.
illustration.alt: A girl sitting still and listening carefully to sounds in the forest

pages.text: She follows the sound. Left along a thick branch. Down through hanging vines. The water sound grows louder. She pushes through curtains of leaves. And there it is. The waterfall. Not where the map said. But real. Silver water tumbles from branch to branch. It catches the light like stars. She dips her hand in the cool stream. The forest kept its secret after all.

illustration.description: Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, and green eyes reaches toward a hidden waterfall that cascades from branch to branch through the emerald canopy. Silver water catches dappled sunlight like jewels. Hanging vines and curtains of leaves frame the magical scene. The illustration combines treasure map style with hand-drawn adventure cartography, using sepia tones with bright water accents.
illustration.alt: A girl discovering a beautiful waterfall hidden in the rainforest canopy

pages.text: Lily sits by the waterfall. She takes out the map. She looks at the wrong path it showed. Then she looks at the right path she found. Maps can lie. But forests always tell the truth. You just have to know how to listen. She pulls out a pencil. She draws a new line on the old map. From the gap to the waterfall. Now the map is right.

illustration.description: Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, and green eyes sits beside the cascading waterfall, carefully drawing a new dotted trail on the aged parchment map with a pencil. The treasure map style illustration shows her correcting the map's route, with compass roses and hand-drawn cartography elements. Emerald canopy light filters through, and silver water sparkles in the background.
illustration.alt: A girl updating an old map with the correct path she discovered

pages.text: She folds the map carefully. Someone else might find it someday. They will see her pencil line. They will find the waterfall too. The canopy holds many secrets. Some are in maps. Some are in the whisper of leaves. Some are in the sound of water over stones. The best treasures are the ones you discover yourself. Lily smiles and starts the journey home.

illustration.description: Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, and green eyes carefully places the corrected aged parchment map back where she found it, tucked under moss on a thick branch. The emerald canopy stretches endlessly around her, full of mystery and possibility. The treasure map style illustration uses sepia tones with jewel accents, showing dotted trails and compass elements as she prepares for her journey home.
illustration.alt: A girl placing a corrected map back in the forest for others to find

pages.text: The sun sets through the emerald leaves. Lily climbs toward home. She knows the canopy better now. She knows it speaks in many voices. Water sounds. Wind sounds. The creak of old branches. Each sound tells her something. Each sound is a clue. She will remember this. Maps are just the beginning of any adventure.

illustration.description: Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, and green eyes climbs confidently through the emerald canopy as golden sunset light filters through the leaves. The vast rainforest spreads below her, mysterious and welcoming. The treasure map style illustration uses sepia and gold tones with compass rose elements, showing her as a bold explorer who has learned the forest's secrets.
illustration.alt: A girl climbing confidently through the forest canopy at sunset

coverPrompt.description: Lily with long curly auburn hair, fair skin with light freckles, and green eyes holds an aged parchment map while standing on a thick branch hundreds of feet above the rainforest floor. The emerald canopy stretches endlessly around her. The illustration is done in treasure map style with sepia tones, compass roses, and jewel-green accents highlighting the lush foliage.
coverPrompt.alt: A girl with auburn curls holding an old map in a vast rainforest canopy

questions.question: Where does Lily find the old map?
questions.explanation: Lily finds the map hidden under moss on a thick branch in the canopy.

questions.question: What is wrong with the map?
choices: It shows a bridge that isn't there
questions.explanation: The map shows a bridge where there is only empty air and a gap too wide to cross.

questions.question: How does Lily find the real waterfall?
choices: She listens to the sound of water
questions.explanation: Lily sits quietly and listens to the forest, hearing the sound of water trickling to the left.

questions.question: What does Lily do to fix the map?
choices: She draws a new line with her pencil
questions.explanation: Lily draws a new line on the map showing the correct path from the gap to the waterfall.

questions.question: What does Lily learn about finding treasures?
choices: The best treasures are ones you discover yourself
questions.explanation: Lily learns that the best treasures are the ones you discover yourself by observing and listening.
